items.title: Charge entry fees to Kunanyi/Mt Wellington, park management told

children.content: A tourism consultancy firm has recommended that visitors to Hobart's Kunanyi/Mt Wellington be charged for access — likely to result in a "deliberate and positive outcome" of fewer people going.

items.title: Rockliff wants to move forward — but will the parliament let him?

children.content: Tasmania's powerful crossbench forced Madeleine Ogilvie and Jane Howlett out the door. Jeremy Rockliff says it's now time to "move forward".

image.altText: A man in a suit speaks at a podium in a room full of politicians seated on green chairs

items.title: Slain officer remembered for caring manner to have laneway named after him

children.content: A Tasmanian police officer who was killed while on duty is remembered for his compassion and care during a memorial service, one year after his death.

items.title: 'I didn't have a life': Pensioner moves to Thailand for cheaper living

children.content: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

children.content: The administrator for the mothballed manganese smelter is continuing to engage with the consortium designated as its preferred buyer, the ABC understands.

items.title: Senator Duniam's resignation could open door for One Nation

children.content: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's announcement that he intends to leave politics could be the leg up that One Nation needs.

items.title: Ex-Hawke minister-turned Catholic priest farewelled in Hobart

children.content: Michael Tate — an academic, politician, diplomat and Catholic priest — has been remembered as a generous man whose life was one of "warmth and service to others".

articles.title: 'We don't take s***': American star's ominous warning to Socceroos

children.content: American midfielder Sebastian Berhalter sends a tough warning to the Socceroos after a physical encounter between the US and Australia in a friendly last year. The two teams meet again on Saturday morning.

image.altText: A man in an American soccer jersey claps at the crowd.

articles.title: A-League referee's 'nonsense' decision denies Mbappe penalty

children.content: Kylian Mbappé scores two more World Cup goals to bring his overall tally to 14, just two shy of the all time record. But the biggest talking point comes when A-League referee Alireza Faghani denies the French superstar a penalty, with the call slammed by experts.

image.altText: Mbappé talks to Alireza Faghani

items.title: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk-off

children.content: Early educators had threatened to walk off the job to keep a federally funded 15 per cent pay subsidy due to end in November.

image.altText: Unidentified child's hands playing with building bricks.

items.title: Jacinta Allan refuses to admit cost of CFMEU corruption to taxpayers

children.content: Embattled Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan has refused to answer repeated questions over how much CFMEU corruption has cost taxpayers in the state, a scandal that has put the government and her leadership under threat.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses holds a document while speaking to reporters in a conference room.

items.title: El Niño declared by BOM and it could become the strongest on record

children.content: El Niño is a state that can linger for up to 12 months and disrupt weather patterns across the globe, with Australia especially prone.

image.altText: A graphic of the Earth with the majority of areas highlighted in yellow and orange indicating warmer weather around Aus.

items.title: Gout runs a stunning third as Lyles breaks world record

children.content: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

image.altText: Two men hug after a running race.

items.title: 'It's all too hard': Fuel shock pushes regional businesses to the brink

children.content: Small-town coffee shops and long-established businesses are at risk of closing despite a ceasefire in the Middle East easing the global oil price. Regional businesses say fuel relief measures need to be extended because the damage done from the fuel shock is far from over.

items.title: Lorne residents hope invention will stop cockatoos from trashing town

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the scenic town.

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

children.content: Russia's defence ministry said it fired the warning shots to divert the yacht that approached on a dangerous course in the English Channel.

items.title: US B-52 bomber plunged into ground at more than 1,500m per minute

children.content: A B-52 bomber aircraft that crashed at a US Air Force base in California, killing eight people aboard, made a sharp right and then nearly completed a 180-degree turn before plunging to the ground.

image.altText: A long black burn mark on an air base runway smouldering with black smoke near parked vehicles.

items.title: 'Life-changing' school for autistic children saved from 'chopping block'

children.content: The private Sycamore School in Alexandra Hills is so sought after, some parents drive almost 50 kilometres one-way to drop off their kids, but it was "on the chopping block".

avatar.alt: A journalist holding a microphone and smiling.

items.title: Time for Russia to make a deal to end Ukraine war, Trump says

children.content: Donald Trump says Moscow should "make a deal" to end the war with Ukraine as G7 leaders have agreed to intensify pressure on Russia.

image.altText: Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Emmanuel Macron smiling.

items.title: FBI says plot to attack White House UFC event with drones thwarted

children.content: US media says five people are in custody in connection with the plot, that involved flying explosive-laden drones towards buildings near last weekend's White House UFC match.

image.altText: A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

items.title: Roadside reptile mystery continues as remains confirmed as saltwater croc

children.content: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

image.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

items.title: How this $500m NSW renewable energy scheme could affect your home

children.content: Interest-free loans and cash subsidies are being offered by the New South Wales government to encourage the uptake of renewable energy measures like solar panels and household batteries.

children.content: Having older parents has shaped my life in many ways, including taking on a carer role for Mum and Dad in my 20s.

items.title: Birth doula says lack of consent delayed Triple Zero call for dying mother

children.content: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

image.altText: Stacey Warnecke smiles at the camera.

items.title: Craft retailer Lincraft to close rest of its stores in Australia and NZ

children.content: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

image.altText: A large white-on-blue 'Lincraft' sign hangs over a storefront, with the tag line 'where ideas come to life'.

items.title: 38yo man jailed for life over 'highly brutal murder' of 22yo wife in NT

children.content: Braden Jentian, 38, has been sentenced to life behind bars for the "protracted and highly brutal murder" of his 22-year-old female partner, whose body was found in a vacant block in Katherine in 2024.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.

items.title: Rabbits are everywhere in the wild, so why is rabbit meat so expensive?

children.content: Once a cheap meat for families doing it tough, the price and availability of rabbit means it has gone from family dinner to fine dining.

image.altText: A rabbit in a paddock.
